name: Gmail / Google

app: Go to your Google Account (myaccount.google.com).
Select 'Security' from the left navigation panel.
Under 'Signing in to Google', select '2-Step Verification'.
Scroll down and click 'Authenticator app'.
Follow the on-screen steps to scan the QR code.
hardware: Go to your Google Account Security settings.
Click 'Security Key' and follow the prompts to register your physical key.

riskIfCompromised: High: Hackers can reset passwords for almost all your other accounts linked to this email.
indianSpecificNote: Many Indians link their bank accounts to their primary Gmail. Securing it is critical.

name: Facebook

app: Go to Settings & Privacy > Settings.
Click on 'Accounts Center' > 'Password and security'.
Select 'Two-factor authentication' and choose your account.
Select 'Authentication app' and follow the setup instructions.

riskIfCompromised: Medium: Scammers can message friends asking for money or run fake ads.

name: WhatsApp

app: Open WhatsApp > Settings.
Tap 'Account' > 'Two-step verification'.
Tap 'Turn on' and enter a 6-digit PIN.
Provide an email address for backup.

riskIfCompromised: High: Attackers can hijack your account to ask contacts for money or access private chats.
indianSpecificNote: WhatsApp hijacking via call-forwarding (*401* scam) is very common in India. The 6-digit PIN prevents this.

name: Instagram

app: Go to your profile > Menu > Settings and privacy.
Go to 'Accounts Center' > 'Password and security'.
Tap 'Two-factor authentication' > select your account.
Choose 'Authentication app (recommended)' and follow the prompts.

riskIfCompromised: Medium: Loss of personal photos, scammers impersonating you.

name: LinkedIn

app: Click your profile icon > Settings & Privacy.
Select 'Sign in & security' > 'Two-step verification'.
Click 'Set up' and choose 'Authenticator App'.
Scan the QR code to finish setup.

riskIfCompromised: Medium: Reputational damage, fake job offers to contacts.

name: Twitter / X

app: Go to Settings and Support > Settings and privacy.
Select 'Security and account access' > 'Security'.
Tap 'Two-factor authentication'.
Check the box for 'Authentication app' and follow instructions.

riskIfCompromised: Low-Medium: Can be used to post crypto scams or phishing links.

name: SBI YONO / NetBanking

app: Banks in India mostly rely on SMS OTPs.
Never share your OTP with anyone.
Avoid downloading any screen sharing apps like AnyDesk when prompted by 'customer care'.

indianSpecificNote: Most Indian banks still enforce SMS OTP. Do not forward OTPs to anyone, even if they claim to be from the bank.

name: Paytm

app: Paytm uses mobile number and OTP/password.
Enable device lock (Pattern/Biometric) in the app settings.
Go to Profile > Security Settings to manage.

riskIfCompromised: High: Scammers can misuse wallet or linked accounts.

name: Zerodha (Kite)

app: Login to Kite web.
Go to 'My Profile' > 'Password & Security'.
Click on 'Enable 2FA TOTP'.
Scan the QR code using Google Authenticator or Authy.

riskIfCompromised: High: Unauthorized trades or liquidation of assets.
indianSpecificNote: SEBI now mandates TOTP or biometrics for trading apps in India. Set it up!

name: Aadhaar Portal (UIDAI)

sms: UIDAI primarily uses SMS OTP to your linked mobile number.

riskIfCompromised: High: Identity theft, taking out loans in your name.
indianSpecificNote: Ensure your biometrics are locked via the UIDAI portal to prevent unauthorized fingerprint access.
